title: 16 Most Popular Long-Haired Dog Breeds Of 2026
description: Are you ready to discover some amazing long-haired dog breeds? These gorgeous pups are not only adorable but also have unique characteristics that make them stand out. Let's take a look at these 16 breeds!

sections.title: 1. Havanese
paragraphs: Havanese have a silky coat that needs daily grooming if kept long. Some owners trim it shorter to reduce brushing time. They are playful and sociable. Havanese are also very intelligent and can be trained easily. They also need regular baths and eye cleaning. These little dogs are perfect for those who want a small, affectionate companion.

sections.title: 2. Shih Tzu
paragraphs: Shih Tzus are famous for their long, beautiful coats. Their charming personalities and affectionate nature make them a popular choice. A long-coated Shih Tzu needs daily brushing, but the coat can be trimmed for lower maintenance, as per the AKC. Shih Tzus are great lap dogs and love to be pampered.

sections.title: 3. Japanese Chin
paragraphs: Japanese Chins have a relatively low-maintenance coat but do shed. Brushing once a week and bathing about once a month are recommended to keep the house cleaner. They are affectionate and make great lap dogs. Japanese Chins are also known for their elegance and grace.

sections.title: 4. Silky Terrier
paragraphs: The Silky Terrier's beautiful coat barely sheds, making it relatively low-maintenance. Regular brushing keeps their coat healthy and shiny. They are lively and spirited. Silky Terriers are brave and make good watchdogs.

sections.title: 5. Chinese Crested
paragraphs: Chinese Cresteds come in two varieties: hairless and "Powderpuff". The Powderpuff needs daily brushing and a bath every few weeks to keep its skin healthy and coat shiny. Chinese Cresteds are very affectionate and love to be with their owners. They are also known for their unique looks.

sections.title: 6. Afghan Hound
paragraphs: The Afghan Hound has a thick, silky coat that protects them in harsh mountainous regions. Their elegant appearance and graceful movements make them a sight to behold. Grooming and exercise needs make them high-maintenance, as noted by Hill's Pet, but their beauty is well worth the effort. Afghan Hounds are independent and regal.

sections.title: 7. English Setter
paragraphs: These energetic hunting dogs have long, silky coats that require weekly brushing. Their coat can have a unique speckled pattern called belton, which no other breed has. They are friendly and active. English Setters are great for outdoor enthusiasts.

sections.title: 8. Rough Collie
paragraphs: The Rough Collie, also known as the long-haired Collie, has a long, smooth coat in various colors. They are intelligent and protective, making them great companions. It requires brushing about once a week to prevent matting, as stated by the AKC. Collies are loyal and gentle.

sections.title: 9. Saint Bernard
paragraphs: Saint Bernards can have both short and long hair. They are famous for their rescue work in the mountains. The AKC recommends weekly brushing and daily brushing during shedding season, which occurs twice a year. Saint Bernards are gentle giants and great family dogs.

sections.title: 10. Newfoundland
paragraphs: Newfoundlands usually have a thick, black coat and need to be kept indoors during warm months. They are known for their strength and swimming abilities. They shed a lot and require regular brushing to prevent matting and aid shedding. Newfoundlands are wonderful water dogs and protectors.

sections.title: 11. Great Pyrenees
paragraphs: The massive Great Pyrenees is a loyal guard dog with a thick coat that mostly self-regulates, requiring minimal brushing. However, they shed heavily at times, so weekly brushing during those periods is helpful. Great Pyrenees are majestic and devoted to their families.

sections.title: 12. Portuguese Water Dog
paragraphs: The Portuguese Water Dog is an athletic breed with "tight, low-shedding curls". They were bred by fisherman to help retrieve gear and herd fish, according to Dog Time, so they require a ton of exercise. Portuguese Water Dogs are intelligent and versatile.

sections.title: 13. Old English Sheepdog
paragraphs: The Old English Sheepdog's double coat requires weekly grooming "down to the skin" and regular brushing even if trimmed to lighten the load. They are hardworking and good-natured. Old English Sheepdogs are charming and make great family pets.

sections.title: 14. Bernese Mountain Dog
paragraphs: Berners originated as farm dogs in Switzerland and have a thick, moderately long coat with three colors. They are known for their loyalty and gentle nature. They shed a fair amount and require weekly brushing during shedding season, according to the AKC. Bernese Mountain Dogs are loving and friendly.

sections.title: 15. Bergamasco
paragraphs: Bergamasco dogs originated in what is now Iran and helped herd sheep. Their unique coat requires a special process of being ripped into mats, but after that, it needs very little maintenance, only bathing a few times a year. Bergamascos are independent and loyal.

sections.title: 16. Maltese
paragraphs: The Maltese is a small dog with a long, silky white coat. They are known for their affectionate and playful nature. Maltese require regular grooming to keep their coat tangle-free and clean. They are also intelligent and easy to train, making them great companions for both individuals and families. Additionally, their small size makes them suitable for apartment living.
